name: Insufficient funds — Nominee fee overdrawn, refund closes the gap
description: Nominee fee is overdrawn by £100.00. Cash in hand before refund is £40.00. After refunding the £100 overdraw, cash in hand becomes £140.00, which is enough to pay the £111.77 creditor shortfall in full. No unrecoverable shortfall. Expected: waterfall triggered, £100 Nominee refund, full £111.77 paid to creditors, zero unrecoverable shortfall.

locked_model.retention_rule: First 10 contributions retained for Nominee fee

expected.final_cashier_instruction_pattern: Refund £100.00 from Nominee fee, then distribute all remaining funds to creditors
expected.omni_fee_notes_pattern: Refund £100.00 from Nominee fee. Remaining cash distributed to creditors

notes: Waterfall: Step A — Nominee overdrawn by £100, refund into case; Step B — cash rises from £40.94 to £140.94; Step C — £140.94 ≥ £111.77 shortfall, pay shortfall in full, remainder to creditors. Figures approximate; validates waterfall logic.
